decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C) by ~4.26°C. Since single-variable tuning has stalled, I will apply a coordinated change to the heat spreader: increasing its conductivity to the maximum bound and slightly widening it to improve lateral heat distribution toward the cooled boundaries.

changes.reason: Maintain maximum conductivity to ensure best possible lateral spreading; previous attempts may not have fully realized geometric benefits.
Widen the heat spreader to its maximum allowed bound (1.0m) to increase the cross-sectional area for heat flow towards the left/right Dirichlet boundaries, reducing thermal resistance.

expected_effects: Reduce peak chip temperature by improving lateral heat spreading efficiency.
Lower thermal gradient across the spreader layer.
Bring chip_max_temperature closer to or below the 85.0°C constraint.
risk_notes: Widening the spreader to 1.0m places it very close to the domain edges; ensure mesh quality remains adequate near boundaries.
Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or vertical transport.
Geometric change might require mesh adaptation in the next simulation step.
